title: Misato and Kajis relationship
wikitext:
[]: Misato & Kaji's relationship             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 10, 2009 11:08 am hey guys, i was watching one of the movies this weekend and the thought occurred to me that i don't really know exactly what went on between Misato & Kaji eight years previous to the series. so, how much detail do we have about that, exactly? i can't, off the top of my head, think of any direct quotes from the series but it was generally my "personal canon" that: a. Misato was somehow attracted to Kaji initially because he reminded her of her father. or, well, she didn't know that at the moment but came to that conclusion in retrospect. b. Kaji wouldn't commit. ("i'll say the words i couldn't say eight years ago...") c. the relationship was very physical. they once missed one whole week of classes at college basically to stay in and have sex. i generally assume Misato was the one who did the breaking up, because of Kaji (this is mostly because of something ritsuko says when Kaji first sees her after arriving from germany, about him possibly having a chance this time around... unless i'm getting myself confused between continuities...). however, then their conversation in ep. 15 happens and Misato mentions outright that it was because of her father issues. but then in EoTV there's such a big emphasis put into the physical aspect of their relationship... was that why she was so pissed at him all the time in the beginning of the series? because she was using sex to avoid dealing with her personal issues and refused to admit it, so she let out her anger at herself on Kaji? (how very Asuka of her). and if Kaji "couldn't say the words," does that mean Misato did ? even with her truckload of issues? so i guess i just want to know... am i missing something here? i feel we got enough info to get a clear picture but for some reason i just can't piece it all together.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 10, 2009 11:40 am The most descriptive dialogue comes from drunken Misato in episode 15, where she compares herself to Shinji, in that when the relationship became serious she ran away because of her fears and anxieties about adulthood.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:39 pm I interpret the mechanics of the relationship thus: Misato is hawt for Kaji. She doesn't know why 8 years ago, and she thoroughly immerses herself in the physical aspects of their relationship. In ep 15 she makes explicit that the emotional ties she feels for Kaji are (perhaps dysfunctionally) related to her father-relationship. I think she may have decided to end the sexual part of the relationship because of that.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 10, 2009 7:36 pm Considering in the series he does do exactly what her father did, neglecting her because of his personal quest and ultimately leaving her holding the bag after he gets himself killed, I'd say she may have been right to drop him the the first time.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 10, 2009 10:03 pm so it was all Misato, then? not Kaji? i keep thinking it her reaction seems more asuka-ish to me. well, maybe not eight years previous-- the running away was all Shinji, certainly, but beginning from episode 8, it's all Asuka. she's not running, necessarily, she's pushing him away. i mean, she calls him everything from a bum to a womanizer when she was the one who dumped him because of her issues. sounds like classic Asuka lashing out at others because she hates herself stuff. yet she never seems to identify with Asuka, not really. and i find that incredibly hilarious for some reason NemZ wrote: Considering in the series he does do exactly what her father did, neglecting her because of his personal quest and ultimately leaving her holding the bag after he gets himself killed, I'd say she may have been right to drop him the the first time.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Aug 10, 2009 10:50 pm I think she may have reserved the "three little words" in the face of a degree of ambivalence - I think she broke it off with Kaji because he wouldn't commit to monogamy (or any other kind of exclusive relationship) with her.

[]:             Quote Post by TheRatherDVD » Tue Aug 11, 2009 2:11 am I think that they did love each other, but were too afraid to commit. So they just absorbed themselves into mindless sex. In other words: It could have gone better.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Aug 12, 2009 11:10 am VoidEater wrote: I think she broke it off with Kaji because he wouldn't commit to monogamy (or any other kind of exclusive relationship) with her.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 12, 2009 12:25 pm Let it be known to all that Carla knows the Misato/Kaji relationship so well it's kind of creepy.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Aug 12, 2009 4:13 pm carla wrote: VoidEater wrote: I think she broke it off with Kaji because he wouldn't commit to monogamy (or any other kind of exclusive relationship) with her.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Wed Aug 12, 2009 4:27 pm From what I recall, I got the impression, from the ending episodes of the TV series/EoE, that: 1) Misato's "cure for everything" is sex. That's her refuge from life's troubles - even though she actually rarely indulges in it (if I recall, she told Kaji she only smokes after sex and he's the only one who knows about her habit). 2) Kaji was similar to Misato's Dad in his kindness, his inability to commit to people, his prioritizing his job/obsession over said people. 3) Misato realized that she was drawn to Kaji exactly because he reminded her of her Father (her Elektra complex). She also felt guilty, thinking she'd only been using him to relieve herself. This freaked her out, leading her to end the relationship - Kaji's inability to commit was probably a more acceptable excuse to herself (and him) than, "Doing it with you was like letting my inner Elektra out to play!"

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Aug 13, 2009 9:40 am AuraTwilight wrote: Let it be known to all that Carla knows the Misato/Kaji relationship so well it's kind of creepy.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Thu Aug 13, 2009 9:59 am carla wrote: if her father couldn't commit to people, then of course she'd be mad at him for that. it's not so much "i'll pin the blame on you because i'm deflecting" but "i'll pin the blame on you because i'm deflecting but you're not mr. perfect either so HAH." (which, in my head and probably only in my head, makes a warped sort of sense).

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Aug 14, 2009 9:43 am LiLi wrote: The thing is, in my understanding the issue of their relationship being physical and its relating to her father issues weren't mutually exclusive in Misato's mind - quite the opposite in fact

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Aug 14, 2009 11:16 am Misato and Kaji never had a meaningful relationship just hot, sweaty sex. They're more fuck buddies than lovers.

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Fri Aug 14, 2009 11:22 am Misato and Kaji never had a meaningful relationship just hot, sweaty sex. They're more fuck buddies than lovers.

[]:             Quote Post by Conzy91 » Sat Aug 15, 2009 4:34 pm Just to offer a different view completely, if we take what he says to be "I would say what I could not 8 years ago, bye." Is this an indication as to her leaving him without giving him a chance to get closure in the relationship, as is revealed in Ep 15 she just left him and there is no indication that she informed him, all she says is that she ran away. This is purely an optional take on things but makes sense seeing as she cites her reason for leaving as he reminded her of her father rather than any commital reasons. Also, I saw someone mention the dreaded argument that it may have been Katsuragi who shot him, this has been officially rebuked by Anno, so we can assume it wasn't her.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 17, 2009 11:59 am Soul Fire wrote: I think her reaction to him dying in the series says other wise as to the depth of thier feelings towards each other.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Aug 17, 2009 12:13 pm carla wrote: what made her hate her father despite the fact that he was her father and so she couldn't hate but love him, was also exactly what made her love Kaji despite the fact that she should hate a guy like him like he hated her father.

[]:             Quote Post by Briecheeze » Thu Aug 27, 2009 10:16 pm Quick clarification - Misato lied about being in love about someone else (at least according to her), presumably to give Kaji a reason for breaking up with him. The real reason was she was sickened that a) she was using Kaji for pleasure, and b) she was dating a man like her father.
